essence: Installs, services, and repairs CNC machines on-site, providing advanced diagnostics and technical support to manufacturing clients.

identity.title: CNC Service Engineer
identity.summary: Travels to customer sites to install CNC machinery, diagnose complex faults, perform repairs, calibrate systems, and ensure machines operate at peak performance.

salary.notes: Engineers specialising in multi‑axis, robotics, or OEM‑level systems can exceed £80k-£100k+ with overtime and travel allowances.

qualifications: Training in mechanical, electrical, or mechatronic engineering
Strong knowledge of CNC machinery, drives, motors, PLCs, and control systems
Ability to diagnose complex mechanical/electrical faults
Experience with installation, calibration, and customer support

lifestyle.description: CNC service engineers work for OEMs, distributors, and engineering service companies. They travel to factories to install, repair, and optimise CNC machines. The role suits individuals who enjoy autonomy, problem‑solving, customer interaction, and working with advanced machinery.

habits: Installs CNC machines and configures control systems
Diagnoses complex mechanical, electrical, and software faults
Performs repairs, calibration, and preventive maintenance
Trains operators and provides technical support
Travels to customer sites and manages service documentation
